summary: v2 export emits no GRANTs on corpus schema; app role gets permission denied
description:
Hit live in prod after merging guru-web and loading v2 corpus artifact:

  error: An error occurred while loading instrumentation hook: permission denied for schema corpus

Cause: export artifact runs as postgres superuser, so the new corpus.* tables are owned by postgres and the app DB role has no rights on the corpus schema. The atomic swap drops corpus_old on each load (along with any GRANTs the operator applied post-load), so the fix has to live in the artifact itself.

Fix:
- export.py reads GURU_DB_APP_ROLE env var (fail loudly if unset), emits GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA corpus_new TO <role> and GRANT SELECT ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA corpus_new TO <role> after the COPY blocks and before the swap. Grants travel with the schema rename.
- guru-web/deploy/README.md: document GURU_DB_APP_ROLE and add to the laptop-side export step (separate PR in guru-web).

Done when:
- export.py emits GRANT statements for the staging schema before the swap
- main() raises SystemExit if GURU_DB_APP_ROLE is unset
- a unit test covers the GRANT emission shape
- prod-style reload as the app role can SELECT from corpus.chunks without permission errors

resolution.note: GRANT USAGE/SELECT emitted on staging schema before swap; APP_ROLE='guru' hardcoded constant matches prod role
